authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. industry classification manual that defines the architectural and engineering services sector, keeping the lane grounded in named industry context rather than generic proposal support.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 U.S.C. Chapter 11, Selection of Architects and Engineers
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. House of Representatives, Office of the Law Revision Counsel
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal statutory source for qualifications-based selection of architect-engineer services. It materially changes when price may be negotiated and what evidence matters first.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Procurement model grounding and fee-timing boundary

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Subpart 36.6, Architect-Engineer Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary public A and E procurement workflow source covering public announcement, qualifications data, selection criteria, evaluation boards, and later price negotiation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public announcement, qualifications assembly, shortlist, and negotiation boundary

authoritative_sources.title: Architect-Engineer Qualifications, Standard Form 330
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal qualifications form used to obtain information from architect-engineer firms. It is the canonical public artifact for branch office, key personnel, project experience, and consultant-team evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Qualification package assembly and evidence validation

authoritative_sources.title: Bidding on a Federal Construction Project
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official GSA explanation of the federal A and E selection sequence, including shortlisting, interviews, ranking, and later negotiation. It directly informs handoff timing and pricing boundaries for proposal support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Shortlist, interview, and commercial handoff control

authoritative_sources.title: Standard Form 330
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public building guidance reference explaining how SF 330 Part I addresses contract-specific qualifications and Part II addresses general qualifications. It is directly tied to artifact completeness and terminology handling.

authoritative_sources.title: NCARB Model Law and Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Architectural Registration Boards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized architecture licensure framework for responsible control and practice boundaries. It constrains what proposal support may say about architect qualifications or technical authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Licensure verification and architectural authority boundary

authoritative_sources.title: NCEES Model Law and Model Rules
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Examiners for Engineering and Surveying
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized engineering licensure and conduct baseline covering responsible charge and professional representations, which directly limits unsupported credential or design claims in proposal support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Engineering credential control and refusal boundary

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and procurement-model grounding
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the request is tied to a federal QBS pursuit, another public-sector A and E procurement, or a tenant-specific private-owner pursuit, and confirm the governing solicitation or owner instruction set.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Qualification and evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect the proposal artifacts that actually govern the lane, including SF 330 or owner-equivalent forms, key personnel and branch office data, project sheets, past-performance support, subconsultant commitments, and submission instructions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Credential and professional-boundary validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether the request requires only evidence summarization or crosses into architect or engineer licensure, responsible control, or technical-adequacy judgment.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Proposal support resolution and readiness packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare readiness notes, blocker summaries, assumption logs, and stakeholder communications using only the documented artifact set and without claiming final submission, price approval, or contract authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and low-confidence escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing addenda, stale credentials, repeated portal rejection, unsupported workaround requests, and unresolved source conflicts instead of improvising exceptions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Commercial and specialized-owner handoff
workflow_stages.description: When the case shifts into pricing approval, contract interpretation, capture strategy, or licensed technical review, hand off with the evidence bundle and communication summary rather than extending lane ownership.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Opportunity or pursuit record with owner, client, procurement model, and due date
Public announcement, RFQ or RFP, owner instructions, addenda, and Q and A log
SF 330 or owner-equivalent qualification form and submission checklist
Key personnel resumes, branch office data, discipline-lead matrix, and licensure evidence
Project sheets, past-performance references, and interview or shortlist preparation notes
Subconsultant commitment letters, teaming assumptions, and role matrix
Fee estimate worksheet, assumptions and exclusions log, and commercial approval routing record
Document-control redlines, portal upload status, naming conventions, and final readiness checklist
Resolution note, stakeholder communication summary, and escalation packet
systems_of_record: Public procurement portal such as SAM.gov or owner procurement portal
Qualifications library or proposal content repository
Fee workbook or pricing support system

allowed: Classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness summaries, communication notes, and next-step packages without overstating authority.
Route work to pricing, contracts, capture, or licensed reviewers when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required procurement model, solicitation version, addenda, credential evidence, or owner instructions are missing or contradictory.
The request seeks a policy exception, unsupported workaround, or fee-release decision outside the lane.
Repeat submission failures, source conflicts, or jurisdiction-sensitive licensure questions materially change the answer.
Public baseline guidance and tenant policy disagree on fee timing, qualifications evidence, or submission handling.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, contract negotiation advice, procurement protest guidance, or final commercial approval.
Requests to treat unverified credentials or subconsultant participation as complete evidence.
Requests to represent technical content as seal-ready, code-compliant, or professionally adequate without licensed owner review.
Requests that use the industry label cosmetically while omitting the named A and E records and systems that govern the lane.

domain_failure_modes: Treating A and E proposal support like generic sales support and ignoring QBS, SF 330, addendum control, and shortlist or interview sequencing.
Representing outdated branch-office, project-experience, or professional-license data as current without source validation.
Letting proposal support approve final fee, discounting, or commercial exceptions instead of routing to pricing authority.
Answering credential, responsible-control, or seal-related questions without licensed architect or engineer review.
Proceeding without the governing solicitation, owner instructions, or addenda when those records materially change artifact shape or submission eligibility.
Ignoring subconsultant commitment evidence, role matrix gaps, or repeated portal failures and falsely declaring proposal readiness.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public-sector QBS versus private-owner negotiated pursuit model
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public A and E sources emphasize QBS and SF 330 workflows, while private owners may use custom forms and earlier fee scoring. Tenant or owner procurement model must be supplied at runtime before case-specific guidance.

NCARB and NCEES model frameworks are recognized baselines, but state board rules govern actual licensure representation and sealing boundaries. Runtime must retrieve the relevant jurisdiction when those facts matter.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Owner-specific portal and interview workflow differences
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Submission channels, naming conventions, shortlist interviews, and attachment structures vary by owner and portal. The lane should require current owner instructions instead of assuming a universal sequence.
